reasoning: The FRED statistical tool provides a combined probability estimate of 0.68 that the spread will exceed 0.91 by April 5, based on a strong upward linear trend. This statistical trend aligns beautifully with the fundamental market narrative discovered via web search: corporate credit spreads hit three-decade lows (~0.71) in January 2026 and have started structurally widening as markets digest policy uncertainty and potential volatility. While short-term spread volatility is high and 7-day intervals can be noisy (implying some mean reversion from the recent single-day jump to 0.91 is possible), the dominant normalization trend points upward. Following instructions to anchor on the statistical estimate, I slightly temper the 0.68 prediction down to 0.66 to account for minor short-term mean reversion risk while respecting the clear structural widening.
